channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why this tribe is buying up hundreds of acres of farmland — and flooding it
item.description: The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.
item.content:encoded:
The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.

(Image credit: Megan Farmer /KUOW)
item.dc:creator: John Ryan

item.title: Timmy the stranded whale rescued after weekslong effort
item.description: Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.
item.content:encoded:
Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.

(Image credit: Sebastian Peters)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Why PEPFAR's top scientist stepped down
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talk with Mike Reid, the former chief science officer of PEPFAR, about why he resigned over concerns about America's global health strategy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talk with Mike Reid, the former chief science officer of PEPFAR, about why he resigned over concerns about America's global health strategy.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline
item.description: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.
item.content:encoded:
More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Giving coffee a jolt could help assess its quality
item.description: NPR's science podcast Short Wave looks at the secrets behind scorpions' weapons, using electricity to measure the quality of a cup of coffee, and what shapes the content of dreams.
item.content:encoded: NPR's science podcast Short Wave looks at the secrets behind scorpions' weapons, using electricity to measure the quality of a cup of coffee, and what shapes the content of dreams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Kwong

item.title: Craig Venter, pioneering human genome decoder, dies at 79
item.description: Pioneering scientist J. Craig Venter has died at 79. His "whole genome shotgun method" helped genome sequencing become faster and cheaper.
item.content:encoded:
Pioneering scientist J. Craig Venter has died at 79. His "whole genome shotgun method" helped genome sequencing become faster and cheaper.

(Image credit: K.C. Alfred/The San Diego Union-Tribune)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: How Trump's EPA head has transformed the agency — and sided with polluters
item.description: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon."
item.content:encoded: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon."
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Like soap operas, 75 feet up: How bald eagle nest cams hook online communities
item.description: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.
item.content:encoded: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: In the midst of an energy crisis, countries make plans to ditch oil, gas and coal
item.description: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.
item.content:encoded:
Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.

(Image credit: Raul Arboleda)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Scientists see Trump's firing of the National Science Board as an attack on research
item.description: The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.
item.content:encoded:
The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman
